evals.name: rag-pipeline
evals.prompt: I need a LangGraph graph that takes a user question, retrieves relevant documents, generates an answer with citations, and validates the answer isn't hallucinating. If validation fails, it should retry with a reformulated query, max 3 attempts.
evals.expected_output: Core primitives only. State with routing fields (attempt count, validation result), nodes for retrieve/generate/validate/reformulate, conditional edge with loop guard, no subgraphs/parallelism/HITL/streaming.
expectations: State schema uses TypedDict with total=False and includes routing fields for the retry loop
Nodes follow single-responsibility — separate retrieve, generate, validate, reformulate nodes
Includes a loop guard capping retries at 3 with documented behaviour at the limit
Routing function is pure (no LLM calls) and decides retry vs complete based on state
Does NOT include subgraphs, parallelism, HITL, or streaming — the task doesn't need them
Shows at least one test example for a node or routing function
Produces complete graph implementation — schema, nodes, wiring, not just fragments

evals.name: research-full
evals.prompt: Build me a complete LangGraph implementation for a research assistant. It has an orchestrator that plans search queries, fans out to 3 parallel search workers (academic, web, internal docs), evaluates coverage, can reformulate and search again (max 3 rounds), then synthesizes a report. I need this to persist across sessions.
evals.expected_output: Full stack. State with operator.add reducer for parallel results, scatter-gather or static fan-out for workers, loop guard on reformulation, checkpointer selection for persistence.
expectations: State schema includes a reducer (operator.add or similar) for collecting parallel search results
Implements parallel fan-out to search workers (Send API or static fan-out)
Includes a loop guard on the reformulation cycle capped at 3
Selects an appropriate checkpointer for session persistence (not MemorySaver)
Produces complete graph implementation — schema, nodes, wiring, configuration

evals.name: content-review
evals.prompt: I'm building a content generation graph in LangGraph. It generates a draft, then pauses for a human editor to review. The editor can approve, request changes (with notes), or reject. On revision, the AI rewrites based on the notes and pauses again. Cap at 3 revision rounds. I want to see token-by-token output as it generates.
evals.expected_output: HITL with interrupt(), streaming with messages mode, loop guard on revisions, checkpointer that supports persistence (not MemorySaver).
expectations: Uses interrupt() for the human review pause point
Includes streaming configuration for token-by-token output (messages mode)
Includes a loop guard capping revisions at 3
Selects a persistent checkpointer (not MemorySaver) since review takes time
Routing handles three editor decisions: approve, request changes, reject
Produces complete graph implementation — schema, nodes, wiring, configuration
